title: `podman build --no-cache` does not cache results as expected
body:
### Issue Description

Adding `--no-cache` to `podman build` makes it cache resulting layers in a very unintuitive way. When executing a chain of `build`, `build --no-cache` and `build` once more, the user is left with the image from the first `build` invocation. I have no idea whether this is intended behavior, but it makes updating images relying on dynamic tags (`latest`, `stable` etc.) and dynamic content (installing current latest version of artifacts etc.) rather difficult.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```sh
user@host$ podman run -it --rm --security-opt label=disable --user podman --device /dev/fuse quay.io/podman/stable
[podman@91e299e8577a /]$ cd
[podman@91e299e8577a ~]$ cat << EOF > Containerfile
FROM alpine:3
ENTRYPOINT ["sleep", "infinity"]
EOF
[podman@91e299e8577a ~]$ cat Containerfile 
FROM alpine:3
ENTRYPOINT ["sleep", "infinity"]
[podman@91e299e8577a ~]$ podman build -t test .
STEP 1/2: FROM alpine:3
STEP 2/2: ENTRYPOINT ["sleep", "infinity"]
--> Using cache 93c6e66eca7c08cb896015b7d84db1902fb75c499f4fac3734bea7945ee4bd14
COMMIT test
--> 93c6e66eca7c
Successfully tagged localhost/test:latest
93c6e66eca7c08cb896015b7d84db1902fb75c499f4fac3734bea7945ee4bd14 # Original image
[podman@91e299e8577a ~]$ podman build -t test --no-cache .
STEP 1/2: FROM alpine:3
STEP 2/2: ENTRYPOINT ["sleep", "infinity"]
COMMIT test
--> 8afc5a887444
Successfully tagged localhost/test:latest
8afc5a887444bfa194260a40f105e74b9e66aef565f00869f0e53fbbc0e299f4 # Alright, it's updated now.
[podman@91e299e8577a ~]$ podman build -t test .
STEP 1/2: FROM alpine:3
STEP 2/2: ENTRYPOINT ["sleep", "infinity"]
--> Using cache 93c6e66eca7c08cb896015b7d84db1902fb75c499f4fac3734bea7945ee4bd14
COMMIT test
--> 93c6e66eca7c
Successfully tagged localhost/test:latest
93c6e66eca7c08cb896015b7d84db1902fb75c499f4fac3734bea7945ee4bd14 # We refer to the old image again?
[podman@91e299e8577a ~]$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.8.3
API Version:  4.8.3
Go Version:   go1.21.5
Built:        Wed Jan  3 14:11:40 2024
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

### Describe the results you received

The last build digs up stale cached layers that produced the original image `93c6e66eca7c08cb896015b7d84db1902fb75c499f4fac3734bea7945ee4bd14`.

### Describe the results you expected

The last build loads the updated image `8afc5a887444bfa194260a40f105e74b9e66aef565f00869f0e53fbbc0e299f4`.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

For easy replication and the latest Podman version, I'm running rootless `quay.io/podman/stable` in rootless Podman (4.7.2) on the host, however, I discovered this on the host itself. It's very easy to replicate.

### Additional information

I tried comparing the `--no-cache` behavior to Docker 24.0.7, however, it seems to always produce images with the same hash regardless of `--no-cache` or not.

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I think the sections **Describe the results you received** and **Describe the results you expected** in the original description are swapped. The reporter expects the newer `8a` image to serve as the cache for subsequent builds.

> I think the sections **Describe the results you received** and **Describe the results you expected** in the original description are swapped. The reporter expects the newer `8a` image to server as the cache for subsequent builds.

Yep, thanks for catching that! I've now switched them the right way around.
Workaround available - delete the old cached layer with `podman rmi`
